blocks.content: She was at her locker. She saw him coming and he watched her decide not to have somewhere to be, which was its own kind of grace.
Same words she would have used a year ago. Different temperature.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I should have told you. Before you heard it from someone else.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I was going to. I just—

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I know. I know I messed up.
She looked at him with the expression she had when she was deciding how much to give and how much to keep.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You knew what that place was.

He didn't say anything. Because there was nothing to say. She was right. He had known. He had stood at the top of that tower and looked out at the town and known, and he had let it happen anyway, and the worst part — the part he couldn't explain and couldn't walk back — was that somewhere on the way up the ladder he had thought of her, and he had done it anyway.
